theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 7 (Electric & Neon) mixed with Archetype 4 (Swiss Brutalist Structure)
theme_and_archetype.emotion: Cutting-edge, premium, technical authority, futuristic but structured

rules: No generic full-screen gradients. Neon is used strictly for accents, interactions, and WebGL particles.
Surfaces must have 1px solid subtle borders.
Light/Dark mode toggle must invert these CSS variables globally.
Headings (Poppins) must use tracking-tight (letter-spacing: -0.04em) and alternate between Light (300) and Black (900) weights for contrast.
Overlines/Labels must be uppercase, tiny, with wide spacing (letter-spacing: 0.2em).
Do NOT center align text unless explicitly inside the Hero or Footer. Left-align everything else.

grid_modes.projects: Asymmetric bento grid. Mix row-span-2 and col-span-2. Never fill 100% perfectly, leave negative space.
grid_modes.services: Strict 3-column grid on desktop, 1-column on mobile. Cards must stretch to same height.

custom_cursor.behavior: Pure JS. Outer ring follows mouse with lerp (delay). Inner dot sticks to pointer exactly.
custom_cursor.hover_state: When hovering over <a> or <button>, the outer ring expands and turns neon magenta.

preloader.visuals: Full screen black (#0A0A0A). Massive '0% - 100%' counter in neon cyan.
preloader.animation: Counter ticks up via JS. Once 100% is reached, GSAP scales the preloader up and fades it out, triggering the Hero animations.

header.layout: Fixed, glassmorphism backdrop-filter: blur(12px). Border-bottom 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1).
header.elements: Logo left, Nav links center, Light/Dark toggle + Contact button right.

hero.webgl: Three.js canvas positioned absolutely behind the text.
hero.animation: Vanilla JS text split into spans -> GSAP stagger text from Y: 100, opacity: 0.

services.layout: Grid of cards with pure CSS 1px border. Background: surface color.
services.animation: GSAP ScrollTrigger. Stagger cards in when scrolling into view.
services.hover: Tracing beam effect or subtle box-shadow neon glow.

projects.interaction: Pure JS Lightbox. Clicking a project dims background and centers the image.

about_tech.timeline: Horizontal scroll section pinned via ScrollTrigger (pin: true). User scrolls down, timeline moves left.
about_tech.marquee: Continuous CSS animation translation of tech icons/words (React, Node, Python, AWS).

testimonials.behavior: Active card is scale(1) opacity(1) z-index(10). Siblings are scale(0.85) opacity(0.5) shifted left/right.

footer.newsletter: Input field with bottom border only. On focus, border transitions to neon cyan.

gsap.rules: Register plugin first. Use gsap.utils.toArray for looping elements. Avoid GSAP premium plugins (no SplitText Club GreenSock; write a custom JS text splitter).

threejs.implementation: Use THREE.Points with THREE.BufferGeometry to create thousands of particles. Animate their Y position and rotation in requestAnimationFrame. Add a slight mouse parallax effect to the camera.

projects.description: Dark laptop showing web app - Use for Project 1
Dark smartphone app showcase - Use for Project 2

abstract.description: Neon abstract lighting - Use for About section or Lightbox filler
Cyberpunk stage - Use for Team or Culture section

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly use Pure HTML, CSS, and JS. Do NOT use React, Vue, or Tailwind. Build vanilla CSS variables for the color system.
Structure the project with index.html, styles.css, and script.js.
Load Google Fonts (Poppins, Inter), GSAP, ScrollTrigger, and Three.js via CDN.
Implement the Light/Dark mode toggle by toggling a '.light-mode' class on the body, overriding the CSS variables.
Since GSAP SplitText is a premium plugin, write a pure JS function to wrap each character/word of the hero heading in a <span>, then animate those spans with gsap.from(..., { stagger: 0.05 }).
Ensure the custom cursor has data-testid='custom-cursor' and elements use appropriate testing IDs.
For the WebGL background, initialize it inside a dedicated <canvas id='webgl-canvas'> behind the hero section. Ensure window resize events update the camera aspect ratio.
The timeline in 'Sobre / Tech Stack' MUST use ScrollTrigger with pin: true to create a horizontal scroll effect as the user scrolls down.
